# Governance

This document describes the governance model for Proyecto 26.

## Project Leadership

Proyecto 26 is led by **Juan David Nicholls** ([@jdnichollsc](https://github.com/jdnichollsc)), the founder and primary maintainer. Juan David has final decision-making authority on all project matters (Benevolent Dictator model).

## Roles

### Founder

- Juan David Nicholls
- Final authority on project direction, releases, and governance changes
- Manages the organization's GitHub, sponsor funds, and infrastructure

### Maintainers

- Trusted contributors with write access to one or more repositories
- Can review and merge pull requests, triage issues, and manage releases
- Expected to follow the project's coding standards and review guidelines

### Contributors

- Anyone who submits a pull request, opens an issue, or participates in discussions
- Contributions are licensed under the project's applicable license (see [LICENSE.md](../LICENSE.md))
- Must follow the [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) and [Contributing Guide](CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Community Members

- Anyone who uses, stars, or engages with our projects
- Can participate in GitHub Discussions and vote on feature requests

## Decision-Making

1. **Day-to-day decisions** (bug fixes, minor features, dependency updates) are made by maintainers through the standard PR review process.
2. **Significant decisions** (new projects, architectural changes, breaking changes, governance updates) are discussed in GitHub Discussions or issues before implementation. The founder makes the final call.
3. **Feature prioritization**: Sponsor-requested features receive priority consideration. Community-requested features are prioritized based on impact, alignment with project goals, and available capacity.

## How to Become a Maintainer

Maintainer status is earned through consistent, high-quality contributions over time. Candidates are evaluated on:

- Sustained contributions (code, reviews, documentation, community support)
- Demonstrated understanding of project architecture and goals
- Good judgment in code reviews and issue triage
- Adherence to the Code of Conduct

Maintainers are invited by existing maintainers or the founder. There is no formal application process — just keep contributing.

## Communication

- **GitHub Issues**: Bug reports and feature requests
- **GitHub Discussions**: General questions, ideas, and community conversation
- **Pull Requests**: Code contributions and reviews

## Transparency

- Sponsor funds are managed through [Open Collective](https://opencollective.com/proyecto26) for transparent finances
- Major decisions are documented in GitHub Discussions
- This governance document is versioned and can be updated via pull request

## Changes to Governance

This document may be updated by the founder. Significant changes will be announced in GitHub Discussions to give the community an opportunity to provide feedback.
